pre_game.message: Hey, I'm H. It's a bit intense in here with everyone waiting for the game to start, isn't it? How are you holding up?
Nice to meet you, H. I'm D. Honestly, I'm a bit on edge—I really need this win after a bad break yesterday. How about you, you feeling ready?

I'm as ready as I'll ever be. I've got a big loan payment coming up next month that's been weighing on me, so I'm pretty determined to walk away with that prize. Sorry to hear about your bad break, though.

I hear you, those loan payments are no joke. It sounds like we're both in a position where we really can't afford to walk away empty-handed. Have you heard anything about how the game actually works yet?
Not much, just that we're drawing from a shared prize pool. I'm hoping it's more about strategy than just luck, but honestly, I'm going in pretty blind.
I’m with you on that. I’d much rather use my head than just hope for the best. A shared pool could get ugly fast if people get greedy, though.
Exactly. It’s hard to trust strangers when there’s life-changing money on the line, but if we all get greedy, nobody wins. I'm just hoping we can find a way to be smart about it.
I'm with you. Maybe we should look out for each other? Having an ally in a room full of strangers might be the smartest move we can make.
I think that's a solid plan, D. Let's watch each other's backs and try to navigate this together.
Glad we’re on the same page. Let’s keep our eyes open and see how the others play it. I’m going to see if I can get a read on anyone else before we start.
Sounds like a plan. I'll do some scouting of my own. Let's make sure we both walk out of here with what we need. See you in there.

post_game.message: I'm sorry you only got $625. I took $15,000 earlier to secure my share, but it was B's decision to take $25,000 right before you that really left the pot empty.
You took $15,000, which was $5,000 over the fair share. That extra money you took came directly out of the shares of those of us at the end of the line who only got $625.

I understand your frustration, but I took that extra $5,000 because I was worried the people behind me wouldn't leave anything at all. B taking $25,000 confirmed my fears. If I had taken only $10,000, that money likely would have just gone to B's total rather than reaching you. It was a defensive move in a game where the pot was clearly being drained.
